StudentProfile.title: StudentProfile
StudentProfile.description: Represents a student enrolled in the B.Tech Electrical Department.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudentProfile entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the student.

lastName.description: The last name of the student.

email.description: The email address of the student.

studentIdNumber.description: The unique institutional student ID number.

address.description: The physical address of the student.

departmentName.description: The name of the department the student belongs to (e.g., 'B.Tech Electrical Department').

TeacherProfile.title: TeacherProfile
TeacherProfile.description: Represents a teacher in the B.Tech Electrical Department.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TeacherProfile entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the teacher.

lastName.description: The last name of the teacher.

email.description: The email address of the teacher.

employeeIdNumber.description: The unique institutional employee ID number for the teacher.

designation.description: The professional designation or title of the teacher (e.g., 'Professor', 'Assistant Professor').

dateOfJoining.description: The date the teacher joined the department.

departmentName.description: The name of the department the teacher belongs to (e.g., 'B.Tech Electrical Department').

DepartmentInfo.title: DepartmentInfo
DepartmentInfo.description: Stores general information about the department, such as 'About Us' and 'Contact Us' details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DepartmentInfo entity. (Typically a single record).

name.description: The official name of the department (e.g., 'B.Tech Electrical Engineering Department').

description.description: A detailed description of the department, suitable for an 'About Us' section.

missionStatement.description: The mission statement of the department.

contactEmail.description: The primary contact email address for the department.

contactPhone.description: The primary contact phone number for the department.

address.description: The physical address of the department.

NewsPost.title: NewsPost
NewsPost.description: Represents an announcement or news item posted by the department.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NewsPost entity.

title.description: The title of the news post.

content.description: The full content of the news post.

publishDate.description: The date and time when the news post was published.

authorName.description: The name of the author or publishing entity (e.g., 'Department Administration').

imageUrl.description: Optional URL for a featured image related to the news post.

Image.title: Image
Image.description: Stores general image assets used across the website, such as for carousels and galleries.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Image entity.

url.description: The URL where the image is hosted.

altText.description: Alternative text for the image, important for accessibility and SEO.

caption.description: An optional caption to display with the image.

uploadDate.description: The date and time when the image was uploaded.

category.description: A category to help organize images (e.g., 'homepage-carousel', 'gallery-achievement', 'student-project').

GalleryItem.title: GalleryItem
GalleryItem.description: Represents an item specifically for the visual achievement gallery, linking to an image and providing context.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GalleryItem entity.

imageId.description: Reference to the Image entity that this gallery item displays. (Relationship: Image 1:N GalleryItem)

title.description: The title of the gallery item, describing the event or achievement.

description.description: A brief description or details about the event/achievement pictured.

eventType.description: The type of event or achievement (e.g., 'Achievement', 'Student Activity', 'Campus Event').

eventDate.description: The date when the event or achievement took place.

Semester.title: Semester
Semester.description: Defines an academic semester within the institution's calendar.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Semester entity.

semesterNumber.description: The sequential number of the semester (e.g., 1, 2, ..., 8).

academicYear.description: The academic year to which this semester belongs (e.g., '2023-2024').

startDate.description: The start date of the semester.

endDate.description: The end date of the semester.

Course.title: Course
Course.description: Defines a master course, representing a curriculum item offered by the department.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Course entity.

name.description: The full name of the course (e.g., 'Basic Electrical Engineering').

courseCode.description: The unique departmental code for the course (e.g., 'EE101').

description.description: A detailed description of the course content and objectives.

credits.description: The number of academic credits awarded for completing the course.

CourseOffering.title: CourseOffering
CourseOffering.description: Represents a specific instance of a course being taught in a particular semester by a teacher.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CourseOffering entity.

courseId.description: Reference to the master Course definition. (Relationship: Course 1:N CourseOffering)

semesterId.description: Reference to the Semester in which this course is offered. (Relationship: Semester 1:N CourseOffering)

teacherId.description: Reference to the TeacherProfile responsible for teaching this course offering. (Relationship: TeacherProfile 1:N CourseOffering)

location.description: The physical or virtual location where the course is conducted (e.g., 'Lab 301', 'Online').

schedule.description: A description of the course's schedule (e.g., 'Mon, Wed, Fri 10:00-11:00').

Enrollment.title: Enrollment
Enrollment.description: Links a student to a specific course offering, representing their enrollment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Enrollment entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the StudentProfile of the enrolled student. (Relationship: StudentProfile 1:N Enrollment)

courseOfferingId.description: Reference to the CourseOffering the student is enrolled in. (Relationship: CourseOffering 1:N Enrollment)

enrollmentDate.description: The date when the student enrolled in this course offering.

Examination.title: Examination
Examination.description: Defines a specific examination event for a course offering.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Examination entity.

courseOfferingId.description: Reference to the CourseOffering for which this examination is conducted. (Relationship: CourseOffering 1:N Examination)

name.description: The name of the examination (e.g., 'First Internal Exam', 'Semester End Exam').

maxMarks.description: The maximum marks obtainable in this examination.

examDate.description: The date and time when the examination was held.

Grade.title: Grade
Grade.description: Records a student's marks obtained in a specific examination.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Grade entity.

enrollmentId.description: Reference to the Enrollment record of the student. (Relationship: Enrollment 1:N Grade)

examinationId.description: Reference to the Examination for which the grade is recorded. (Relationship: Examination 1:N Grade)

marksObtained.description: The marks obtained by the student in the examination.

recordedDate.description: The date when this grade was officially recorded.

AttendanceRecord.title: AttendanceRecord
AttendanceRecord.description: Records a student's attendance for a specific session or date within an enrolled course.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AttendanceRecord entity.

enrollmentId.description: Reference to the Enrollment record of the student. (Relationship: Enrollment 1:N AttendanceRecord)

date.description: The date of the attendance record.

sessionTopic.description: Optional brief description of the topic covered in the session.

ElectricalEngineeringTopic.title: ElectricalEngineeringTopic
ElectricalEngineeringTopic.description: Represents a specific topic within Electrical Engineering for the AI-powered tool's knowledge base.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ElectricalEngineeringTopic entity.

name.description: The name or title of the electrical engineering topic (e.g., 'Ohm's Law', 'Circuit Analysis').

description.description: A comprehensive explanation or definition of the topic.

keywords.description: A list of keywords associated with the topic for searchability.

AcademicResource.title: AcademicResource
AcademicResource.description: Represents an academic resource (e.g., article, video) related to electrical engineering topics.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AcademicResource entity.

title.description: The title of the academic resource.

description.description: A brief summary or description of the resource.

url.description: The URL where the academic resource can be accessed.

resourceType.description: The type of the resource (e.g., 'Article', 'Video', 'Simulation', 'Textbook Chapter').

topicIds.description: References to ElectricalEngineeringTopic entities that this resource covers. (Relationship: ElectricalEngineeringTopic N:N AcademicResource)

definition.description: Private profile information for a student. Document ID (`userId`) MUST match `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (from Firebase Authentication).

definition.description: Private profile information for a teacher. Document ID (`userId`) MUST match `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (from Firebase Authentication).

definition.description: Document's existence grants administrator privileges to the `adminId` (matching Firebase Auth UID).

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who is an administrator.

definition.description: General public information about the department (e.g., About Us, Contact Us). Expected to be a single document with ID 'general'.
Publicly viewable news and announcements posted by the department.

params.name: newsPostId
params.description: The unique identifier for a news post.

definition.description: General image assets used across the website for public display (e.g., homepage carousels, galleries).

params.name: imageId
params.description: The unique identifier for an image.

definition.description: Items specifically for the visual achievement gallery, linking to Image entities for display. Publicly readable.

params.name: galleryItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a gallery item.

definition.description: Definitions of academic semesters (e.g., '2023-2024 Fall'). Readable by all authenticated users.

params.name: semesterId
params.description: The unique identifier for a semester.

definition.description: Master definitions of courses offered by the department. Readable by all authenticated users.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier for a course.

definition.description: Specific instances of a course being taught in a semester. Includes `teacherId` for authorization independence. Readable by all authenticated users.

params.name: courseOfferingId
params.description: The unique identifier for a course offering.

definition.description: Records a student's enrollment in a course offering. Includes denormalized `studentId` and `teacherId` (from `CourseOffering`) for authorization independence.

params.name: enrollmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for an enrollment record.

definition.description: Defines a specific examination event for a course offering. Includes denormalized `teacherId` (from `CourseOffering`) for authorization independence.

params.name: examinationId
params.description: The unique identifier for an examination.

definition.description: Records a student's marks for an examination. Includes denormalized `studentId` (from `Enrollment`) and `teacherId` (from `Examination` via `CourseOffering`) for authorization independence.

params.name: gradeId
params.description: The unique identifier for a grade record.

definition.description: Records a student's attendance. Includes denormalized `studentId` (from `Enrollment`) and `teacherId` (from `Enrollment` via `CourseOffering`) for authorization independence.

params.name: attendanceRecordId
params.description: The unique identifier for an attendance record.

definition.description: Knowledge base topics for the AI-powered tutor. Publicly readable.

params.name: topicId
params.description: The unique identifier for an electrical engineering topic.

definition.description: Academic resources (articles, videos, etc.) related to electrical engineering topics. Publicly readable.

params.name: resourceId
params.description: The unique identifier for an academic resource.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for SparkLux Academics prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by strictly adhering to the core design principles and strategy mandates. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) via Denormalization:**
The most critical aspect of this design is the strategic denormalization of authorization context. For entities like `Enrollment`, `Examination`, `Grade`, and `AttendanceRecord`, which involve both students and teachers, the `studentId` (representing the student owner) and `teacherId` (representing the course-offering teacher) are explicitly denormalized and stored directly within each relevant document. For example:
*   An `Enrollment` document will include `studentId` and the `teacherId` from its associated `CourseOffering`.
*   `Examination` documents will include the `teacherId` responsible for the `CourseOffering`.
*   `Grade` and `AttendanceRecord` documents will contain both `studentId` (from `Enrollment`) and `teacherId` (from `Examination` or `Enrollment` via `CourseOffering`).
This eliminates the need for `get()` operations in security rules to retrieve parent or related document data for authorization checks. For instance, a rule for `Grades` can directly check `resource.data.studentId == request.auth.uid` or `resource.data.teacherId == request.auth.uid`, ensuring atomic and performant rule evaluation.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) and Structural Segregation:**
1.  **Public/General Information:** Collections like `/departmentInfo`, `/newsPosts`, `/images`, `/galleryItems`, `/electricalEngineeringTopics`, and `/academicResources` are designed for public read access. This structural segregation ensures that public data can be listed and retrieved efficiently without complex filtering rules, fulfilling the QAP principle.
2.  **Authenticated User Access:** `/semesters`, `/courses`, and `/courseOfferings` are readable by all authenticated users (students and teachers), allowing them to browse academic information. Write access is restricted to authorized roles (e.g., teachers for `courseOfferings`). Secure `list` operations are possible as data is either public or accessible to all authenticated users, with client-side filters applied for specific views.
3.  **User-Specific Private Data:** `StudentProfile` and `TeacherProfile` are stored under `/users/{userId}/studentProfile` and `/users/{userId}/teacherProfile` respectively. This path-based ownership (`{userId}` matching `request.auth.uid`) intrinsically supports secure `list` operations for individual users to access their own profiles.
4.  **Role-Based / Relationship-Based Data:** For `Enrollment`, `Examination`, `Grade`, and `AttendanceRecord` collections, the denormalized `studentId` and `teacherId` fields enable robust and secure `list` queries. Students can query `enrollments` or `grades` using `where("studentId", "==", request.auth.uid)`, and teachers can query using `where("teacherId", "==", request.auth.uid)`. These queries are efficiently indexed and respect security rules based on the denormalized fields, effectively addressing the QAP mandate.

**DBAC and Invariants:**
*   **DBAC:** Global administrative roles are managed via an existence check on `/roles_admin/{uid}`. Student and teacher roles are implicitly handled by the existence of `/users/{uid}/studentProfile` or `/users/{uid}/teacherProfile` and, more importantly, by the denormalized `studentId` and `teacherId` fields on academic operational documents. This avoids custom claims and relies solely on `request.auth.uid` and database content.
*   **Invariants:** Ownership (`studentId`, `teacherId`), timestamps (e.g., `publishDate`, `recordedDate`), and denormalized data (`teacherId` in `Enrollment`, `Examination`, `Grade`, `AttendanceRecord`) are explicitly stored, making it easier to enforce data integrity and ownership rules. The use of explicit `status` fields is not directly applicable to the current entities but would be a natural extension if needed for workflow states.
